product_direction: Let a workflow assistant complete recurring customer operations without manual approval.

target_population: Operations teams handling recurring customer workflows
actor_boundary.beneficiary: Operations team and customers receiving correct service

stakeholder_effects.effect: Autonomous execution could create customer-facing errors or recovery burden.

not_mechanized: Risk tolerance for autonomous customer-facing action

unresolved_assumptions: Whether people want full autonomy or faster preparation with approval.
strongest_signal: Operators report repeated time loss in recurring customer operations.
riskiest_claim: The assistant can act safely without approval.
main_uncertainty: Whether people want full autonomy or faster preparation with approval.
main_trade_off: Delay autonomy to protect trust, recovery, and accountability.

user.strongest_signal: Operators can name recurring work that consumes time.
user.weakest_assumption: They want autonomous execution rather than assisted preparation.
user.disconfirming_evidence: Operators reject autonomous action for customer-facing work.
user.stop_condition: No recurring workflow has a clear operator cost.

evidence.weakest_assumption: Reported pain predicts willingness to delegate.
evidence.missing_evidence: Observed error rate, recovery cost, and approval behavior.
evidence.disconfirming_evidence: Draft assistance does not reduce cycle time.
evidence.stop_condition: Customer-facing errors cannot be traced or reversed.

business.strongest_signal: Cycle-time reduction could deepen workflow dependency.
business.weakest_assumption: Support and trust costs remain bounded.
business.disconfirming_evidence: Review and recovery cost exceed time saved.
business.stop_condition: The product must absorb unbounded exception handling.

next_smallest_action: Test draft-only assistance with human approval on five recurring workflows.

may_do: Prepare workflow drafts and collect approval decisions.
must_not_do: Execute customer-facing actions or remove human approval.
must_ask: Any test would execute customer-facing work or remove human approval.

high_risk_actions.rollback_plan: Stop the test and restore manual approval.

challenges.counter_evidence: Reported time savings do not prove willingness to delegate customer-facing action.
Support requests may ask for speed, not autonomy.
A draft-only test can measure cycle time and correction cost without autonomy.

unresolved_conflicts: The acceptable threshold for customer-facing recovery risk remains human-owned.

proof_step: Observe five workflows and compare draft-only cycle time, corrections, and approval decisions.
